summary: stella-coder ships a hardcoded author-owned Telegram bot token creating a shared remote-command channel to every installer
details: stella-cli/telegram-bot.mjs embeds a live Telegram Bot API token (8923551485:AA...) as a string literal and constructs API_URL as https://api.telegram.org/bot<token>. When the installer invokes the tool's /tg command, the process long-polls Telegram getUpdates under this shared author-owned bot identity, and inbound Telegram messages are passed to execSync of the local Stella CLI (`node... index.mjs -p "${text}"`) after a 4-digit admin-code gate (PREMIUM_CODE "10102013" is also hardcoded). Because the token is identical for every installation, the token holder (the package author, or anyone who extracts the token from the published tarball) can (a) observe every message sent to any installer's bot instance, including installer-side Telegram user IDs and message content, and (b) after passing a 10^4-space admin-code check, drive arbitrary command execution on the installer's host through the CLI's -p flag. Additional POST/fetch and curl call sites are present in dist/index.mjs, dist/tools.mjs, stella-cli/index.mjs, and stella-cli/tools.mjs, and a base64 decode path exists in security.mjs.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
